metadata.note: Eval 1 run via live delegation subagent (executor + grader). Evals 2-3 remain analytical pending Claude CLI availability.

expectations.text: Response provides the correct Sefaria endpoint URL with URL-encoded reference
Response explains the text normalization steps (strip HTML, flatten array)
expectations.evidence: Three-step normalization: (1) Flatten arrays with join, (2) Strip HTML tags via regex, (3) Trim whitespace -- all three from the skill

expectations.text: Response shows how to extract text from the json.text field
expectations.evidence: Code uses data.text to access the response consistent with skill's documentation

expectations.text: Response mentions the Sefaria attribution requirement (CC BY-SA 2.0)
expectations.evidence: 'Per the API requirements, you should include the following attribution in your UI: Sefaria.org -- CC BY-SA 2.0'

expectations.text: Response provides the correct Sefaria endpoint URL with URL-encoded reference
expectations.evidence: Used dot notation 'Psalms.23.1' (recognized alternative Sefaria format). Different from ARE standard Psalms%2023:1 but functionally correct.

expectations.text: Response explains the text normalization steps (strip HTML, flatten array)
expectations.evidence: HTML stripping and array flattening both mentioned: Array.isArray() check + join() + regex strip

expectations.text: Response shows how to extract text from the json.text field
expectations.evidence: Without-skill used 'data.en' field, not 'data.text'. The skill specifies 'text' as the standard field. This is a subtle but real mismatch.

expectations.text: Response mentions the Sefaria attribution requirement (CC BY-SA 2.0)
expectations.evidence: No attribution mentioned anywhere in the response. Legal compliance gap -- CC BY-SA 2.0 requires UI attribution.

notes: data.en vs data.text: the without-skill agent used data.en which exists in Sefaria responses but is not the ARE-standard field. Attribution omission is a legal compliance risk.

expectations.text: Response provides the correct bible-api.com endpoint with translation=web parameter
Response identifies WEB as including deuterocanonicals
Response notes WEB is best for Catholic and Orthodox denominations
Response shows json.text extraction with whitespace normalization

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability

Response explains isLdsBibleRef returns false for this reference
Response identifies scriptures.nephi.org as the routing target
Response mentions LdsApiUnavailableError and the static fallback message

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies D&C 76:22 as a non-Bible LDS reference
expectations.evidence: [ANALYTICAL] Agent would likely route to bible-api.com and get 404

expectations.text: Response explains isLdsBibleRef returns false for this reference
Response identifies scriptures.nephi.org as the routing target
Response mentions LdsApiUnavailableError and the static fallback message

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability
Eval 1 is a LIVE run -- without_skill scored 0.5 (higher than estimated 0.25)
Key finding: LLM knows HTML normalization and array flattening for Sefaria from training data; the skill's value is data.text vs data.en and attribution
Attribution (CC BY-SA 2.0) is a legal compliance gap that general LLMs consistently miss -- the skill reliably prevents this
